assessor_1_raw: Card fraud detection: well-designed with chargeback-confirmed labels, temporal split, precision at fixed recall primary metric, and comprehensive ablation suite. Both models receive equivalent tuning. SHAP analysis for interpretability is appropriate for a fraud system. Verdict: defense_wins.
assessor_2_raw: Sound fraud detection experiment. The bidirectional LSTM/transformer on historical sequences is appropriate (no lookahead). Merchant embedding ablation directly tests the key confound. Geographic and temporal stratification are good confound controls. Verdict: defense_wins.
assessor_3_raw: The experiment correctly uses chargeback-confirmed labels (90-day resolution) to avoid label noise. The precision at fixed recall primary metric aligns with business objective. AUPRC secondary metric provides full curve context. Bootstrap CIs and temporal stratification address stability concerns. Verdict: defense_wins.
adjudication_raw: Unanimous defense_wins from all three assessors.
